Article Title: Google for Startups partners Antler India to launch Google for Startups Immersion in India: What is it
Article Text:
Representative Image

Google for Startups Immersion : Eligibility and selection criteria



Google for Startups Immersion: Two-phase programme with technical and business tracks



Phase 1 — Online Immersion (June 2, 4, 9, 11, 2026):

Phase 2 — Offline Immersion (June 26, 2026):





Google for Startups has partnered with Antler India to introduce the Google for Startups Immersion programme in India. This initiative is aimed at founders and technical leaders building AI-focused startups with live products. Applications for the programme opened on May 8 and will close on May 22, according to an official announcement.The two-phase hybrid programme is designed for startups that already have a minimum viable product (MVP) and early traction, with a focus on improving how they build and scale using artificial intelligence tools and business frameworks.The programme is open to founders, CTOs, and technical leads who already have a deployed product and verifiable traction. Startups at the idea stage are not eligible.Participants will be evaluated on their ability to demonstrate practical use cases of AI tools covered during the sessions. Around 50 startups will be shortlisted for interviews, from which 25 to 30 will be selected for the final offline immersion.Key dates and timeline* Applications open: May 8, 2026* Applications close: May 22, 2026* Online sessions: June 2, 4, 9, 11, 2026* Offline shortlist announcement: June 15, 2026* Offline immersion: June 26, 2026The programme reflects a growing focus on applied AI development in India’s startup ecosystem, combining technical training with business guidance for companies already in execution.The programme will be conducted in two stages: an online immersion, followed by a selective in-person cohort.This phase will involve up to 5,000 people from around the country over two weeks, with four virtual sittings. The technical track will include training on technologies such as AI Studio, Antigravity, and the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, with Google for Startups as the lead partner in delivering the content. The business track will be organised by Antler India, focusing on AI investment trends and defensibility.A shortlist of around 25 startups will be invited to attend an in-person session at Google Ananta. Selection will be based on performance in the online phase, followed by rapid interviews. The offline event will include architecture whiteboarding sessions with Google Cloud engineers, one-on-one mentoring with Antler’s team, and closed-door discussions.
Article Summary: Google for Startups Immersion is aimed at founders and technical leaders building AI-focused startups with live products. The two-phase hybrid programme is designed for startups that already have a minimum viable product (MVP) and early traction. The programme will be conducted in two stages: an online immersion, followed by a selective in-person cohort.

Article Text:
Cuban says AI responses a reality check for ‘doomers’



Google, Uber CEOs say AI-generated code is approved by engineers







Billionaire investor Mark Cuban has a warning for the corporate world, and specifically those who have a view that AI will become an all-powerful entity that takes over the world: Your AI is only as good as the person checking its work. In a recent comment on social media platform X about the state of Enterprise AI, Cuban argued that the technology’s greatest weakness is its lack of consistency, which makes deep industry expertise more valuable than ever.Cuban said that AI models are currently incapable of providing the same answer to the same question every time. Unlike traditional software, which follows a rigid logic, Large Language Models (LLMs) produce answers that are based on probability theory. They essentially ‘guess’ the next word or action, which can lead to different results in every session, the Shark Tank star said.“I’m coming to the conclusion that the biggest challenge for Enterprise AI, and AI in general , as of now, is that it’s still impossible to make sure that everyone gets the same answer to the same question, every time,” Cuban noted, noting that for businesses that rely on precision, this ‘unreliability’ is a massive liability.Cuban also used this technical limitation to address the “AI Doomers” – critics who fear that AI is on a fast track to gaining consciousness and taking over the world. He said that if an AI doesn't understand that its output should be consistent, it certainly doesn't understand the real-world consequences of what it's saying.“[This] is a great response to the doomers. AI doesn’t know the consequences of its output. Judgement and the ability to challenge AI output is becoming increasingly necessary, and valuable,” he addedCuban threw his weight behind those who have domain knowledge – deep, specialised expertise in a specific field.“Which makes domain knowledge more valuable by the second,” he added.Google CEO Sundar Pichai and Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi, in their companies’ respective earnings calls, backed AI-generated code. Pichai said that the company generates about 75% of codes by AI models but they are approved by human engineers.“We've been using AI to generate code internally at Google for a while. Today, 75% of all new code at Google is now AI-generated and approved by engineers, up from 50% last fall,” Pichai said in a blog post, adding, “Every line of that code is reviewed and approved by engineers.” Khosrowshahi said that about 10% of code is written by AI but it is approved by engineers.
Article Summary: Billionaire investor Mark Cuban says AI models are incapable of providing the same answer to the same question every time. Unlike traditional software, which follows a rigid logic, Large Language Models (LLMs) produce answers that are based on probability theory. ‘Unreliability’ is a massive liability for businesses that rely on precision.

Article Title: Former US Army CIO Leonel Garciga says that the tension playing out with US Army's rapid adoption of AI technologies is ...
Article Text:
Image courtesy: LinkedIn

Leonel Garciga also highlighted US Army’s biggest problems



AI adoption creates whiplash



Leonel Garciga Joins Booz Allen







The US Army is reportedly racing to integrate artificial intelligence and digital tools across its operations. However, the former chief information officer Leonel Graciga feels that the real challenge isn’t the technology, it’s the people. “The hardest part is never the tech, ever,” Garciga told Business Insider. “It’s getting workers to rethink how and why they do things, and encouraging them to play with new tools in a sandbox with some supervision.” During his three-year tenure, Garcia pushed through red tape and accelerate experimentation. His philosophy was simple: roll out tools quickly, prioritise user experience and accept the risk of failure. “Let’s just make it ubiquitously available and see what happens. Let’s break some glass,” he said.This approach ran counter to the military’s traditional yearlong development cycles, but Garciga argued that speed was important to keep pace with evolving threats and technologies.As per the Business Insider report, Garciga also highlighted that many of the Army’s biggest problems stemmed from poor user experience.Solders often waited for weeks for the access to the systems and also struggled with outdated memo formats and faced excessive paperwork for routine tasks. Even basic communication tools frustrated troops. One of the most popular requests he received was for help turning messy memos into properly formatted documents.The Army’s rapid deployment of AI tools has created a sense of whiplash among personnel. Soldiers and civilians are being asked to experiment with new platforms, but many feel overwhelmed. “Probably the biggest demand signal we get is, ‘Hey, how do I get trained on this because I don’t understand what I’m looking at?’” Garciga said. Policy gaps add to the confusion, as governance struggles to keep pace with technology.Recently, Garciga joined Booz Allen as a Senior Executive Advisor. In this role, Garciga will leverage his leadership, experience, and passion for providing technology to the warfighter to enhance the company’s delivery of defense technology products and solutions in alignment with Department of War priorities.“After a remarkable career with the Army, I’m excited to bring my passion for reimagining technology’s role in mission success to Booz Allen, where I can continue to accelerate the convergence of technology, data, and vision to solve real operational challenges,” Garciga said. “My mission remains the same: delivering capability at scale, faster, for no-fail missions. I look forward to harnessing the power of Booz Allen’s technical innovation and mission understanding to safeguard America. ”
Article Summary: US Army is reportedly racing to integrate artificial intelligence and digital tools across its operations. Former chief information officer Leonel Graciga feels that the real challenge isn’t the technology, it’s the people. During his three-year tenure, Garcia pushed through red tape and accelerate experimentation. Garciga has joined Booz Allen as a Senior Executive Advisor.

Article Title: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet on Corporate America problem: All the data is showing companies are investing in AI, but …
Article Text:
“Actually, that was a playbook.”

How Accenture has done internal restructuring to reflect broader industry shift

“We changed what the ship looked like. We really have completely changed ourselves, multiple times,”

“Think of all the uncertainty that creates,”

Poll Do you believe integrating AI into corporate operations is a significant challenge for companies today? Yes No





Accenture CEO Julie Sweet has flagged a growing gap in Corporate America’s approach to artificial intelligence (AI). She noted that while companies are increasing investments in AI, many are still struggling to fully integrate it into their operations and workforce. Her comments come as Accenture undergoes internal changes to align its structure and talent strategy with AI-driven transformation. Sweet pointed out that organisations are allocating resources toward AI, but translating those investments into measurable transformation remains a challenge. At Accenture, this has led to a restructuring of its operating model and workforce strategy, including tying employee promotions to AI proficiency.Talking to Fortune from the backstage of the Great Place to Work For All Summit in Las Vegas, Sweet, referring to the decision to make AI fluency a requirement for career progression, saidShe compared the shift to earlier digital transitions, noting that the pace of AI adoption is moving faster than previous technology cycles.To adapt, Accenture has reworked its organisational structure, moving away from a long-standing service- and geography-led model to one designed for continuous, AI-led reinvention.The company has also introduced a talent strategy focused on reskilling employees while exiting roles where adaptation is not feasible.Sweet said, describing the transformation.The company has incurred restructuring charges as part of this transition while continuing to expand its global workforce. Sweet emphasised that such changes require transparency to maintain employee engagement.she said. Good communication has helped employees stay on task during the transition, she added.Accenture’s strategy is built around embedding AI skills across its workforce. Promotions are now linked to AI understanding, as the company sees future roles becoming increasingly reliant on these abilities.Sweet said the approach mirrors earlier digital transformations but is being implemented more quickly. Unlike previous shifts, where evaluation systems changed after initial adoption, AI-related expectations are being integrated earlier in the process.
Article Summary: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet has flagged a growing gap in Corporate America’s approach to artificial intelligence. She noted that while companies are increasing investments in AI, many are still struggling to fully integrate it into their operations and workforce. Her comments come as Accenture undergoes internal changes to align its structure and talent strategy.

Article Title: Europe wants to bring new rules restricting VPNs; says VPNs have a 'huge problem' that allows…
Article Text:
The EU is grappling with VPNs, viewing them as a loophole to circumvent new age-verification systems for adult content. This comes as millions use VPNs to bypass these measures, mirroring trends seen after similar laws in the UK and Florida. Privacy advocates warn against restricting VPNs, citing Russia's failed attempts to block them and Utah's new location definition.

The more countries enforce age checks, the more people download VPNs

Privacy groups say this path leads somewhere dangerous





The European Union has a VPN problem—and it knows it. The European Parliamentary Research Service, a research arm of the European Parliament, published a briefing this week describing VPNs as "a loophole in the legislation that needs closing. " The trigger: millions of people are using the privacy tools to bypass new age-verification systems designed to keep children off adult websites.The timing is pointed. EU Executive Vice-President Henna Virkkunen had just told a press conference that circumventing the bloc's newly launched age-verification app through a VPN should not be possible, calling it part of the "next steps" policymakers need to examine. She stopped short of saying "ban," but not by much.The pattern is almost comically consistent. After the UK's Online Safety Act kicked in, one app developer reported an 1,800% jump in downloads in a single month, per Tom's Hardware. Proton VPN saw 1,400% more signups. Florida saw a 1,150% VPN surge within hours of Pornhub blocking access there. The EPRS paper acknowledges this openly—it just doesn't have a clean answer for it.Adding to the EU's embarrassment: its own age-verification app was found storing facial scan data in unencrypted files, with its biometric check bypassed in under two minutes by a security researcher flipping a single setting. CyberInsider first reported the EPRS briefing that followed.Mozilla, Mullvad, and Proton fired back with a joint letter to UK officials last week, warning against moves that would "undermine the open internet." The VPN Trust Initiative put it bluntly: treating VPNs as a loophole is "a complete misunderstanding of their role."Across the Atlantic, Utah became the first US state to legally define a user's location by physical presence—not IP address—effectively calling VPN use irrelevant. Digital rights groups called it unenforceable. Experts point to Russia's years-long, expensive, and largely failed attempt to block VPNs as a cautionary tale.No concrete EU legislation is on the table yet. But the direction is getting harder to misread.
Article Summary: The EU is grappling with VPNs, viewing them as a loophole to circumvent new age- verification systems for adult content. This comes as millions use VPNs to bypass these measures, mirroring trends seen after similar laws in the UK and Florida. Privacy advocates warn against restrictingVPNs, citing Russia's failed attempts to block them.

Article Title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on what excites him to most when it comes to robotics and physical AI: ‘We need a…’
Article Text:
‘We need a digital twin’



Why it matters







Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has shared a bold vision for the future of artificial intelligence (AI) innovation, suggesting that the next great frontier for computing isn’t just on our screens, but in the physical world around us. During a keynote at the recently-held Adobe Summit, sitting down with Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen, Huang discussed how Nvidia is pivoting toward “Physical AI”, technology that allows computers to understand and interact with the tangible world.When Narayn asked what excites the Nvidia founder about what's happening with physical AI Innovation and robotics, Huang said that while AI has mastered digital data, the biggest industries remain largely physical, to revolutionise these sectors, computers must first learn the laws of the physical world.“The vast majority of the world is physical, and if we want to be able to apply computing for the first time to some of the largest industries in the world – whether it’s life sciences or logistics and manufacturing or transportation – unless the computer can understand the physical world, there is no chance of enhancing it, no chance of automating again,” he told Narayan.“Many of the people in this room are producing products, and they're marketing those products, but those products are very specific that product needs to be precise… the brand identity has to be precise… the design is precise.It's not an approximate representation of the product and so many of the things that we do needs to have a perfect digital twin. That starting point cannot be negotiable,” Huang explained.“We need a high fidelity, truthful, in its most precise representation, which is 3D graphics, digital representation of the artifact,” he added.“It could be a car it could be, you know, perfume bottle, it could be a person…whatever it is. From there, we can then integrate it with generative AI and express our creativity through that,” Huang noted, emphasising that these “digital twins” serve as a bridge, allowing generative AI to interact with the real world.By creating these precise 3D models, companies can test robots in a virtual space before putting them on a factory floor, simulate transport systems to find efficiencies without real-world risks, and even merge creativity with AI by using digital artifacts as a canvas for new designs.
Article Summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has shared a vision for the future of artificial intelligence (AI) Innovation and robotics. Huang said that while AI has mastered digital data, the biggest industries remain largely physical. To revolutionise these sectors, computers must first learn the laws of the physical world. ‘We need a digital twin’

Article Title: Apple strengthens Mac security, widens bug bounty push
Article Text: Apple is strengthening macOS security with a mix of built-in malware protections, behavioural detection systems and expanded safeguards against social engineering attacks, while also widening its security research and bug bounty efforts.Modern macOS protections go beyond traditional anti-virus approaches by combining cryptographic system sealing, app notarisation and XProtect, Apple’s built-in anti-malware technology. The notarisation allows it to identify malicious software and infrastructure before malware is widely deployed, helping block threats earlier in the attack cycle.XProtect, integrated directly into macOS, now includes both signature-based and behavioural detection along with automated remediation capabilities. This gives it multiple ways to respond to malware campaigns, including blocking malicious apps, revoking developer certificates and updating XProtect signatures.With cybercriminals increasingly relying on social engineering instead of conventional malware delivery methods, Apple has also introduced new safeguards in macOS Tahoe 26.4. Attackers are increasingly attempting to trick users into manually pasting commands into Terminal to install infostealer malware and bypass native Mac protections.To counter this, macOS 26.4 introduces new warnings when relatively inexperienced users paste commands into Terminal. Apple has also added new XProtect signatures to detect malicious scripts and expanded Terminal-based alerts tied to known harmful sources. The warnings are disabled during the first 24 hours of setting up a new Mac and for users with developer tools such as Xcode installed, though warnings linked to known malicious content will always appear.Apple has also updated FileVault recovery handling in macOS 26.4 by moving recovery keys into the end-to-end encrypted Passwords app. According to the company, this reduces the risk of recovery keys being exposed or lost.Separately, Apple has started rolling out “Background Security Improvements” across macOS, iOS and iPadOS. Introduced with macOS 26.3.1, the mechanism allows the company to push smaller security fixes and protections for components such as Safari, WebKit and system libraries between full software updates.Alongside platform security updates, Apple continues to expand its bug bounty program, which rewards security researchers for identifying vulnerabilities across Apple products and services. The company said the programme has evolved to cover a wider range of attack categories and security research areas as threats targeting Apple devices become more sophisticated. The rewards have been almost doubled by the Apple for those who find bugs across its ecosystem.
Article Summary: macOS Tahoe 26.4 adds built-in malware protections, behavioural detection systems and expanded safeguards against social engineering attacks. Apple has also widened its security research and bug bounty efforts. The company said the programme has evolved to cover a wider range of attack categories and security research areas.

Article Title: Mother’s Day 2026: Smartphones, speakers, earbuds and more gadgets from Samsung, OnePlus, Realme and other brands that you can gift
Article Text:
Samsung Galaxy S26



Nothing Phone 4(a) Pro

Sennheiser Accentum Open Sakura Pink Edition

Noisefit Diva Arya

OnePlus Nord 6

Realme 16 5G

Boat Chrome Iris smartwatch

Dell 15

GoBoult Mustang GT20 speaker





Mother’s Day is observed on the second Sunday of May every year. The day is celebrated to honour and appreciate the love, care, and sacrifices of mothers. This year, the day falls today, i.e. May 10, 2026. If you are still looking for the perfect gift, gadgets could be a great option. From smartphones and wireless earbuds to smart speakers and fitness trackers, tech gifts are both useful and thoughtful. Brands like Samsung, Sennheiser, OnePlus, Realme, and others have a wide range of devices that can make for a special present. Here’s a look at some of the best gadgets you can gift your mom this Mother’s Day.Samsung Galaxy S26 features a 6.3-inch FHD+ Dynamic AMOLED 2X display with support for an adaptive refresh rate ranging from 1Hz to 120Hz. The smartphone is available with up to 12GB RAM and storage options of 256GB and 512GB. The model carries an IP68 rating for water and dust resistance and supports Wi-Fi 7 connectivity.The Galaxy S26 features a 4,300mAh battery with 25W wired charging. Wireless charging and Wireless PowerShare are supported on the device.Samsung Galaxy S26 has a triple rear camera setup that includes a 50MP primary camera with optical-quality zoom, a 12MP ultra-wide sensor, and a 10MP telephoto lens with 3x optical zoom.The smartphone is available in colour options including Cobalt Violet, White, Black and Sky Blue, along with Samsung.com exclusive Pink Gold and Silver Shadow variants. Price of Galaxy S26 starts at Rs 87,999.Nothing Phone 4(a) Pro has a 6.83-inch 1.5K 144Hz AMOLED display peaking at 5,000 nits brightness. It's powered by the Snapdragon 7 Gen 4 paired with up to 12GB LPDDR5X RAM with UFS 3.1 storage. Camera-wise, the Pro gets a 50MP main shooter with a Sony LYT700c sensor and OIS, a 50MP 3.5x periscope telephoto with OIS and up to 140x ultra zoom, an 8MP ultra-wide, and a 32MP selfie camera.The Glyph Matrix returns with 137 mini-LEDs covering a 57% larger area than the Phone (3) version, hitting 3,000 nits. The Indian variant packs a 5,400mAh battery compared to the 5,080mAh global unit, with 50W wired charging that gets to 60% in 30 minutes. Price of the Phone (4a) Pro starts at Rs 39,999.Bright, lightweight, and beautifully designed, the Accentum Open Sakura Pink Edition has an open-ear design and custom 11 mm dynamic transducers. The earbuds are claimed by the company to deliver clear, balanced audio. The device comes with Bluetooth 5.3 multipoint connectivity, intuitive touch controls, and up to 28 hours of battery life with the case making it as reliable as it is stylish. This makes ACCENTUM Open Sakura Pink ideal for:• Moms who want a stylish, everyday audio companion• Busy routines, from home to errands and everything in betThe earbuds debuted with a price tag of Rs 9,990 last year, but can easily be purchased on Amazon India for approximately Rs 6,990 to Rs 7,990.Noisefit Diva Arya is a jewellery-inspired ceramic watch, priced at Rs 4,999 in India. The smartwatch comes with a glass-like finish and 60 precision-set stones, giving it a design that leans toward traditional jewellery. It is also available in metal and mesh strap variants for users looking for different styles.The Diva Araya features an AMOLED display with up to 850 nits of brightness, helping maintain visibility in outdoor conditions. The ceramic variant offers scratch resistance and a polished finish, while the embedded stones enhance its visual appeal.On the functionality side, the smartwatch includes features such as cycle tracking with phase-based insights and overnight skin temperature tracking. It also offers an SOS feature with live location sharing, aimed at providing an added layer of safety. Additional features include Bluetooth calling and support for over 100 customisable watch faces, allowing users to personalise the interface based on their preferences.OnePlus Nord 6 is powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 chipset. It features a 165Hz 1.5K AMOLED display with up to 1800 nits high brightness mode and up to 3600 nits peak HDR brightness. The phone is backed by a 9,000mAh silicon-carbon battery with 80W SUPERVOOC charging support. It also offers bypass charging for sustained performance and 27W reverse wired charging for powering other devices.For photography, the device comes with a 50MP Sony main sensor with dual-axis optical image stabilisation and a 32MP front camera. It supports 4K video recording at 60fps and includes AI-based tools such as AI Eraser, AI Unblur, AI Reframe, and AI Detail Boost.The Nord 6 runs on OxygenOS 16 and includes AI features such as real-time translation, AI Writer, AI Scanner, and integration with Google Gemini. It also introduces AI Mind Space, which allows users to save and organise on-screen content. The pricing for the Nord 6 starts at Rs 38,999.Realme 16 5G comes in two colours, Air White and Air Black. It comes with a starting price of Rs 31,999. The handset has an advanced Dual 50MP Portrait Camera System featuring a 50MP front camera and a Sony IMX852 50MP rear camera. The smartphone comes with a 6.57‑inch compact display and an ultra‑narrow 2.23mm bottom bezels. It is backed by 7,000mAh Titan Battery supported by the first‑in‑segment Titan Long‑Life Algorithm.Realme 16 5G comes with IP69 Pro-level water resistance, along with IP66, IP68, IP69, and IP69K certifications. The device runs Realme UI 7.0 with the new Flux Engine.Boat Chrome Iris comes with a price tag of Rs 3,499 and is available in Metal Gold, Rose Pink, Velvet Wine, Silk Skin, Frosted Sky colour options. The smartwatch comes with a 8mm slim metal build and it features a 1.32-inch AMOLED display with 466x466 pixel resolution. The display offers up to 1000 nits of peak brightness level. The company claims that high brightness levels ensure effortless readability even under direct sunlight. With Always-On Display support, the Chrome Iris keeps essential information just a glance away.Boat Chrome Iris smartwatch comes with Bluetooth calling functionality and is equipped with heart rate monitoring sensor, SpO2 tracker and sleep tracking functionality. The smartwatch also offers multiple sports modes and the IP68 rating makes it resistant to dust and water.Dell 15 comes with a starting price tag of Rs 69,699. The laptop runs on Intel Core Ultra 2 series H processors built on Arrow Lake architecture, with options for the Core Ultra 5 225H or Core Ultra 7 255H. It integrates Intel Arc graphics and a dedicated NPU to handle AI workloads efficiently, ensuring smooth multitasking without draining battery life.Dell 15 features a 15-inch FHD display with up to 300 nits brightness, a three-sided narrow bezel, wide viewing angles, and an anti-glare coating. ComfortView software reduces harmful blue light, making long hours of use more comfortable. For communication, it includes an HD webcam, integrated microphones, and AI-powered noise reduction for clear video calls. The laptop is backed by a massive 13,380 mAh battery, supported by ExpressCharge technology that delivers up to 80% charge in just one hour.Connectivity is enhanced with a full-function USB Type-C port and wireless options, ensuring compatibility with a wide range of peripherals and displays.GoBoult Mustang GT20 comes with a price tag of Rs 2,999, with a market operating price of Rs 3,999 and an MRP of Rs 7,999. It is available in Turbo Ice and Turbo Black colour options. The Mustang GT20 features a 20W output, dual dynamic drivers, and a passive bass radiator. It includes design elements such as a metallic race grille, ignition-inspired shift-stick controls, fuel-gauge battery indicator, and underbody RGB lighting. The speaker supports Bluetooth 6.0 and TWS pairing, offers up to 7 hours of playback, and is IPX4 water-resistant.
Article Summary: Mother’s Day is observed on the second Sunday of May every year. This year, the day falls today, i.e. May 10, 2026. From smartphones and wireless earbuds to smart speakers, tech gifts are both useful and thoughtful. Brands like Samsung, Sennheiser, OnePlus, Realme, and others have a wide range of devices that can make for a special present.
